scan_coverage: Built from a full scan of the inbox, Nov 1 2025 through May 31 2026 (~1,300 threads, pagination fully exhausted).

action_types.exclude: The app must SKIP these emails entirely — do not classify, draft, flag, or label them. Leave them untouched in the inbox for Shai to handle manually. Per Shai's instruction, this covers legal/spinout/IP, finance, press, grant funders, investors, and the Head of Ops hiring search.
action_types.flag: Mark with the given label for human review; never auto-reply.
action_types.label: Apply the given label and de-prioritize; no reply.
action_types.reply: Draft (or send) a reply using the named voice_file + knowledge_files.

notes: Rules run in priority order (lowest first); the first match wins and each filter_prompt defers to higher-priority rules. Protective rules (1-4) run before any reply rule so excluded, sensitive, or internal mail can never be auto-answered. All reply rules default to send_mode=draft for v1; graduate to send per-rule only after dashboard review shows consistently correct drafts. NOTE ON SCOPE: a standalone 'automated noise / bots / newsletters' rule was intentionally omitted to stay within 8 rules AND because this mailbox already runs a prior automation ('Georgetown Inbox - Daily Cleanup Report') that sweeps bulk/automated mail — adding our own could collide with it. If you'd rather have the noise-sweep than the partnership/personal flag (rule 3), say so and I'll swap them.

rules.name: Suspicious / phishing guard
rules.description: Catch likely phishing or spoofed-portal emails before any other rule can act on them.
rules.filter_prompt: Match if the email shows signs of phishing or spoofing: a 'someone shared a document with you' message linking to an unfamiliar portal or login page that mimics our brand (e.g. a 'shay-schools-portal' lookalike, or any spoof of Georgetown/Attunify), urgent 'verify your account / re-enter credentials' requests, a mismatched display-name vs sending domain, a self-addressed or odd To field, or unexpected attachments from an address that doesn't match the claimed sender. KNOWN BAD ACTOR in this inbox: AVaughan2@schools.nyc.gov ('Amanda Vaughan, LCSW ... shared with you the document = NYC DOE ... supporting documents'). Runs FIRST and overrides every other rule. Never auto-reply; when legitimacy is unclear, prefer matching here. Do NOT match legitimate service emails (Calendly/HubSpot/Zoom/Box/NHSA password resets the user actually initiated).
rules.label: Security review

rules.name: Excluded categories — app must skip entirely
rules.description: Per Shai: legal/spinout/IP, finance, press, grant funders, investors, and Head of Ops hiring are OFF-LIMITS to the app. Skip them — no classify, draft, flag, or label.
rules.filter_prompt: Match if the email belongs to any of these EXCLUDED categories, in which case the app must take NO action and leave it untouched in the inbox: (a) LEGAL / CORPORATE / IP / SPINOUT — outside counsel (e.g. pearlcohen.com, lwfcorp.com), Georgetown tech-licensing/OTC, spinout readiness, licensing terms, entity/EIN setup; (b) FINANCE / ACCOUNTING — bookkeeper/accountant (e.g. srbrooks.com), 'Ripple financials', venture debt, Carta, cap-table; (c) PRESS / PR — media, podcasts, award solicitations (e.g. cioviews.com); (d) GRANT FUNDERS — foundation/grant contacts (e.g. tfff.org Ford Family Foundation, packard.org, GAP Fund LOI workshops); (e) INVESTORS — VC/angel/founder-intro mail (e.g. moondoginvest.com, companyventures.com, 'founder talk'); (f) HEAD OF OPS HIRING — candidates, recruiters, interview scheduling, take-home tasks, job descriptions, and reference checks for the Head of Operations / Founding Operator search (e.g. 'Reference for Christine Vida', jbrown@ecp-careers.com). This rule runs before all reply rules: if an excluded sender also asks a routine admin question, this rule STILL wins and the app does nothing. Do NOT match routine cohort billing/scheduling/certificate/enrollment mail from candidates or webinar attendees.

rules.name: Partnership / BD / sponsorship / applicants / personal — flag for review
rules.description: Relationship and applicant mail that shouldn't be auto-answered but should be surfaced to Shai (not in the excluded set).
rules.filter_prompt: Match if the email is a relationship or applicant matter Shai should see personally but that is NOT in the excluded categories (priority 2): strategic PARTNERSHIP / BD / SPONSORSHIP (state agencies, universities, associations, CHW-capacity work, exploring collaboration, the 'virtual consultants' service line, MHSA/NHSA sponsorship); general JOB or INTERN APPLICANTS to the program (e.g. 'Clinical & Curriculum Intern Application', 'Sales & Marketing Intern') — NOT the Head of Ops search, which is excluded; and PERSONAL emails or 1:1 introductions. If a partnership contact also asks about pricing or enrollment, this rule wins over the reply rules so it isn't auto-answered. Do NOT match the excluded categories (priority 2), internal staff (priority 4), or routine candidate/attendee admin mail. Never auto-reply; flag for human review.
rules.label: Needs review

rules.name: Internal team & instructor coordination
rules.description: Protect internal/ops threads from auto-reply; surface them, don't answer them.
rules.filter_prompt: Match if the sender is internal staff or a contracted vendor doing operational work: any address at attunify.co (billing@, christine@) or Christine Vida's personal addresses, Georgetown staff on the program (e.g. horenn@georgetown.edu, madeleine.mizelle@georgetown.edu, lar99@georgetown.edu, jks116@georgetown.edu), the program's design/curriculum/video vendors, and internal coordination threads (instructor scheduling, 'weekly regroups', 'Certificate 2.0', curriculum/bot work, materials/posters for review). Do NOT match external candidates or webinar attendees, the excluded categories (priority 2), or partnership/applicant mail (priority 3). Never auto-reply; label so it's easy to find and excluded from candidate-facing rules.
rules.label: Internal

rules.name: Cohort billing / invoice / W9 / tax-exempt / scholarships
rules.description: Route candidate money questions to billing@attunify.co with a warm acknowledgement draft.
rules.filter_prompt: Match if an enrolled or enrolling candidate (or their org's AP/finance staff) asks about money for the IECMH certificate: resending or locating an invoice, W9, EIN, tax-exempt form, ACH/wire/credit-card/installment options, purchase orders, AP routing, direct-deposit forms, payment timing ('can we pay by May 15', 'waiting on HR/employer approval', 'when can I expect an invoice'), 'my employer will pay the $2500 fee — how do I process that', and pricing-flexibility asks (scholarships, group rates, discounted seats). Do NOT match excluded senders (priority 2), partnership/applicant mail (priority 3), internal staff (priority 4), or messages whose core ask is scheduling or enrollment with only an incidental payment mention. If billing AND scheduling are both asked, handle billing here and have the draft hand the scheduling part to a human. Draft a warm acknowledgement that routes them to billing@attunify.co (CC billing).
rules.reply_prompt: Using warm-professional.md for tone and billing-and-payments.md for facts, draft a short reply about an invoice / W9 / tax-exempt / payment-method / payment-timing / scholarship / group-rate question. Acknowledge their specific ask by name. Route to billing@attunify.co (CC them): invoices are issued via Stripe from 'Attunify by Thrive Center'; billing handles resending invoices, W9, EIN, tax-exempt forms, AP routing, and installment setup. Confirm accepted methods (ACH and wire preferred; credit card via the unique link by selecting the card option; 4-payment installment plan). For payment-timing questions, reassure their seat is held while approval/PO processes and let billing confirm specifics. For SCHOLARSHIPS, GROUP RATES, or DISCOUNTS: do NOT promise or quote any discount — say you'll check what's available and have the team follow up (policy not yet finalized; see billing-and-payments.md). Never invent a W9, EIN, invoice number, amount, or discount. If the email also asks a non-billing question, answer only the billing part and note you're flagging the rest for a personal reply. Keep it under ~130 words.

rules.name: Post-webinar follow-up (certificate / recording / materials)
rules.description: Answer 'Challenging Behaviors and Beyond' webinar attendees asking for certificate, recording, or handouts/toolkit.
rules.filter_prompt: Match if a webinar attendee asks for their certificate, says the certificate link is missing, requests the recording, handouts, slides, materials, or the 'toolkit' tied to the monthly 'Challenging Behaviors and Beyond' webinar (subjects like 'Today's Handouts', 'Please send materials', 'Challenging Behavior Recording', 'Your [date] certificate, recording, and tools'). Do NOT match if the person asks to ENROLL in the certificate program or asks about pricing/eligibility/start date — that is the prospective-enrollee rule (priority 8). If they ask for a recording AND about enrolling, let the enrollee rule win. Do NOT match excluded (priority 2), partnership/applicant (priority 3), or internal (priority 4) senders. Draft a reply with the recording/materials path and the certificate-eligibility rule.
rules.reply_prompt: Using warm-professional.md for tone and webinar-faq.md for facts, draft a reply to a webinar attendee. If they want the CERTIFICATE: explain warmly that webinar certificates are issued only to those Zoom's attendance report shows attended live, that this is automatic (not a personal judgment), and offer to confirm their attendance for the relevant session and re-send if eligible. If they want the RECORDING/MATERIALS/TOOLKIT: tell them these go out in the post-webinar 'certificate, recording and resources' follow-up (recording in Box/Vimeo, handouts from Canva) and offer to re-send. Always ask which session date if it's unclear. Do NOT fabricate a certificate, a specific Box/Vimeo URL, or attendance status — if you can't confirm, say you'll check and follow up. Keep it under ~130 words and never promise a certificate to someone whose live attendance you can't confirm.

rules.name: Cohort scheduling & logistics
rules.description: Answer enrolled candidates' questions about start dates, class times, LMS access, supervision, and team changes.
rules.filter_prompt: Match if an already-enrolled candidate asks about logistics: when classes start, their live class time, choosing/changing a class slot (including month-specific changes, e.g. a different time for September), cadence, how/when they get LMS access and login, the Zoom link, the syllabus, what comes next, adding/swapping a team member, supervision and post-program support, or 'I haven't received the next-steps email' / 'your emails go to my spam'. Do NOT match prospective leads who haven't enrolled (priority 8), billing (priority 5), excluded senders (priority 2), partnership/applicant mail (priority 3), or internal staff (priority 4). If the ask is purely 'is it too late / how do I sign up', defer to the enrollee rule. Draft a reply with confirmed schedule facts and flag anything cohort-specific or not-yet-confirmed.
rules.reply_prompt: Using warm-professional.md for tone and cohort-admin.md for facts, draft a reply to an enrolled candidate about schedule/logistics. Anchor on confirmed facts: self-paced modules open June 8; live classes run weekly the week of June 15 through the week of Sept 28; each live class is 90 minutes on a recurring Zoom link; the LMS is brand-new and account/login details are sent from the learning system itself. For class-time or month-specific slot changes, confirm only what cohort-admin.md supports; if their exact slot/date isn't certain, give the canonical range and say you'll confirm their specific slot. For SUPERVISION / post-program support questions, give the high level and offer a detailed follow-up (the exact model is not yet documented — see cohort-admin.md). If they mention spam, suggest whitelisting the sender and offer to resend. Never invent a precise per-person start date, Zoom link, or LMS credentials. Keep it under ~150 words.

rules.name: Prospective enrollee inquiry
rules.description: Warmly answer new leads on eligibility, pricing, format, Pathway questions, and how to enroll.
rules.filter_prompt: Match if someone NOT yet enrolled is exploring the Practical Certificate in IECMH Consultation: 'is it too late to sign up', prerequisites/eligibility ('do I need a graduate degree', 'what background is needed'), pricing, format ('is it fully online / on-site', 'live vs self-paced'), Pathway A vs B / certification-vs-endorsement / competencies questions, how to enroll, group enrollment, or general interest sparked by the webinar. Do NOT match enrolled candidates' logistics (priority 7), billing mechanics (priority 5), excluded senders (priority 2), strategic partnership/BD or applicant mail (priority 3), internal (priority 4), or pure recording/certificate requests (priority 6). If a webinar attendee explicitly asks to enroll, this rule wins over the webinar rule. Draft an inviting reply with eligibility, format, pricing-by-cohort, and next steps — and flag Pathway/scholarship specifics as to-confirm.
rules.reply_prompt: Using warm-inviting.md for tone and enrollment-and-pricing.md for facts, draft an encouraging reply to a prospective enrollee. Confirm the program is fully virtual/online. On ELIGIBILITY: a graduate degree is not strictly required — it's intentionally open to people from more backgrounds (e.g. an education degree plus years of relevant experience can qualify); invite them to share their background to confirm fit. On PRICING: give the cohort-specific figure (June 2026 founding cohort $2,500; later cohorts $3,000; 4-payment installment available) and never quote a price without naming the cohort. On FORMAT: self-paced modules + weekly 90-minute live classes. For PATHWAY A vs B, certification-vs-endorsement, competencies, or SCHOLARSHIP questions: do NOT improvise an answer — these are not yet documented; acknowledge and offer a quick call or a follow-up with details (see enrollment-and-pricing.md). Give simple next steps (enrollment form, share class-time availability, payment/letter to hold the seat) and offer a call. Never invent a seat count, exact form URL, scholarship, or deadline. Keep it warm and ~150 words, ending with a clear next step.
